pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'Save the formatted file as Formatted_Script.docx in the Documents folder'
Task description and industry standards. Web search confirms Courier New is an acceptable variation of the industry-standard Courier 12-point screenplay font, used by screenwriting professionals.
Unit conversion verification: 12 points = 12 × 2 = 24 half-points. Task description requires '12pt' font, which converts to 24 in half-point units as used in Word XML.
Unit conversion verification: 1.0 inch = 1.0 × 1440 = 1440 twips. Task description specifies 'Top: 1.0"'. Standard Word unit is twips (1/20th of a point, 1440 per inch).
Unit conversion verification: 1.0 inch = 1.0 × 1440 = 1440 twips. Task description specifies 'Bottom: 1.0"'.

Unit conversion verification: 1.5 inches = 1.5 × 1440 = 2160 twips. Task description specifies 'Left: 1.5"'. This margin value aligns with standard screenplay formatting (1.5" left margin for binding).
Unit conversion verification: 1.0 inch = 1.0 × 1440 = 1440 twips. Task description specifies 'Right: 1.0"'.

Unit conversion verification: 2.0 inches = 2.0 × 1440 = 2880 twips. Task description specifies 'Character Names: Left Indent 2.0"'. Web search indicates industry-standard character name position is 3.7" from page left, which equals 1.5" (left margin) + 2.2" (relative indent). Task uses 2.0" relative indent, positioning characters at 3.5" from page left, close to the 3.7" standard.

Unit conversion verification: 1.0 inch = 1.0 × 1440 = 1440 twips. Task description specifies 'Dialogue: Left Indent 1.0"'. This places dialogue at 2.5" from page left (1.5" + 1.0"), which matches the industry-standard dialogue position of 2.5".
Unit conversion verification: 1.5 inches = 1.5 × 1440 = 2160 twips. Task description specifies 'Dialogue: Right Indent 1.5"'.

Unit conversion verification: 1.5 inches = 1.5 × 1440 = 2160 twips. Task description specifies 'Parentheticals: Left Indent 1.5"'. This places parentheticals at 3.0" from page left (1.5" + 1.5"), close to the industry-standard range of 3.1-3.4".

File system verification: Raw_Script_Scene.docx was not found in /examples/microsoft_word_starter_env/data/ directory. File does not exist in create_docx_files.py script. Task expects this file to be available, but it is missing from the actual environment.

pi_items.metadata_value: setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 referenced in hooks
pi_items.source: File system verification: Task.json references hooks for 'setup_task.ps1' and 'export_result.ps1' at C:\workspace\tasks\format_screenplay_scene\, but these files do not exist in the task directory. Other similar tasks (contract_amendment_track_changes, clinical_trial_protocol_structure) have these scripts present.

Web search verification: Microsoft Word 2010 is a real product, released to manufacturing April 15, 2010, with general availability June 15, 2010. It is version 14 of Word. Confirmed in environment configuration (env.json specifies 'Microsoft Word 2010 from Office 2010 Pro Plus ISO').

pi_items.metadata_value: Task indentation values match screenplay formatting convention
pi_items.verified_value: Partially aligned with industry standards, with minor deviations
pi_items.source: Web search (StudioBinder, NFI, Final Draft, industry sources): Standard screenplay formatting uses character names at 3.7" from page left (task: 3.5"), dialogue at 2.5" from page left (task: 2.5" - exact match), parentheticals at 3.1-3.4" from page left (task: 3.0" - slightly below range). Task margins and font (Courier New 12pt) align with screenplay industry standards.

privileged_info_summary: The task format_screenplay_scene@1 targets Microsoft Word 2010 (a real product released June 15, 2010) and implements screenplay formatting standards including Courier New 12-point font, specific page margins (1.0" top/bottom, 1.5" left, 1.0" right), and character-specific indentations that align closely with industry standards. All metadata unit conversions (points to half-points, inches to twips) are mathematically correct. However, the task has critical missing components: the input data file 'Raw_Script_Scene.docx' does not exist in the data directory and is not generated by the data creation script, and the required setup/export PowerShell scripts referenced in task hooks are absent from the task directory. These missing files would cause task execution to fail.
